headline: US-Iran war enters third week with 6,000+ targets struck, Hormuz remains mined as IEA approves historic 400M-barrel reserve release, Israel expands Lebanon ground offensive past the Litani River, and USTR launches unprecedented dual Section 301 investigations against 76 trading partners.

top_stories.title: US-Iran War Week 3: 6,000+ Targets Struck, Kharg Island Oil Terminal Destroyed, New Supreme Leader Vows Continued Resistance
top_stories.summary: The US-Israeli war against Iran entered its third week with no ceasefire in sight. CENTCOM reported approximately 6,000 targets struck since operations began on February 28. Iran reported 1,348 civilians killed and over 17,000 injured, including up to 175 students killed when the Pentagon admitted striking a girls' school in error. Mojtaba Khamenei assumed the role of Supreme Leader after his father was killed by Israeli strikes and has adopted an even more hardline stance. Trump stated the US military had 'totally obliterated' targets on Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export terminal, potentially destroying the country's oil export capacity for years.
top_stories.investor_impact: War entering an extended phase with no political pathway to resolution. Iran's oil export infrastructure destroyed, removing approximately 1.5 million bpd from global supply permanently until rebuilt. US defense spending unchecked after Senate failed to pass war powers resolution. Six US troops killed; war costs mounting alongside $175B tariff refund liability.

top_stories.title: Hormuz Blockade Persists as IEA Approves Largest Strategic Reserve Release in History
top_stories.summary: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei pledged to maintain the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, declaring 'not a litre of oil' would pass through. Three more commercial ships were struck in the Gulf this week. In response, the IEA approved release of 400 million barrels from strategic petroleum reserves — the largest coordinated drawdown in the agency's 50-year history, far exceeding the 183M barrel release during the 2022 Ukraine crisis. The US will contribute 172M barrels over 120 days. Brent crude remains above $100/bbl at $103.86, with WTI at $99.34 (up 8.86% weekly).
top_stories.investor_impact: Historic SPR drawdown signals the severity of the energy crisis but can only partially offset the ~8 million bpd supply loss from Hormuz. US gas prices at $3.63/gal (22-month high). Energy-importing nations face severe current account pressure. Inflation pass-through from energy shock expected in March and April readings. Mine clearance remains a months-long prospect even after hostilities cease.

top_stories.title: Israel Expands Lebanon War Past Litani River — 680+ Killed, 800,000 Displaced
top_stories.summary: Israel dramatically expanded military operations in Lebanon, advancing past the Litani River with evacuation orders reaching the Zahrani River. Over 680 people have been killed and 800,000 displaced. Israel struck Hezbollah financial institutions and conducted over 250 strikes in a single day, killing at least 50 people. Lebanon's PM demanded Hezbollah surrender all weapons to the state and restrict itself to political activities only — a historic shift in Lebanese government policy. Hezbollah launched retaliatory strikes on northern Israel.
top_stories.investor_impact: Lebanon faces a state-failure scenario with massive reconstruction costs ahead. Hezbollah disarmament demand, if enforced, would fundamentally reshape Lebanese politics. Regional insurance and shipping risk elevated. Israeli economy under multi-front war strain with GDP trend in strong decline.

top_stories.title: USTR Launches Unprecedented Dual Section 301 Investigations Against 76 Economies
top_stories.summary: The US Trade Representative initiated two sweeping Section 301 investigations: one targeting 16 economies over structural excess manufacturing capacity across 21 sectors including semiconductors, EVs, batteries, and steel; and a second targeting 60 trading partners over failures to enforce bans on goods produced with forced labor. Public hearings are scheduled for late April and early May. The combined scope represents the most aggressive US trade enforcement action in decades.
top_stories.investor_impact: Could result in a broad new tariff regime affecting global supply chains across electronics, automotive, clean energy, and manufacturing. Asian exporters face significant exposure. Combined with the ongoing Iran war, this represents a dual economic shock — energy supply disruption plus trade policy uncertainty.

top_stories.title: North Korea Fires 10 Ballistic Missiles During US-South Korea Exercises; France Deploys 10 Warships to Hormuz
top_stories.summary: North Korea fired approximately 10 ballistic missiles toward the eastern sea in its largest single salvo, timed to the US-South Korea Freedom Shield exercise involving 18,000 troops. Kim Yo Jong warned of 'unimaginably terrible consequences.' Separately, France announced an unprecedented naval deployment — the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, 8 frigates, and 2 amphibious helicopter carriers — to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after Iranian drones struck Cyprus, bringing the Middle East conflict to EU territory for the first time.
top_stories.investor_impact: US military capacity is being tested across multiple theaters simultaneously — Iran, Korean Peninsula, and now French-led Hormuz reopening. South Korean defense spending and won volatility elevated. European defense stocks benefiting from French deployment. NATO-adjacent operations in Gulf signal deeper European security commitment.

regional_summaries.North America: The US is stretched across multiple fronts: the Iran war entering its third week with no congressional check after the failed war powers vote, dual Section 301 trade investigations against 76 economies, and a razor-thin House majority (217-214) after Rep. Kiley switched to independent. February CPI was benign at 2.4% but pre-dates the energy shock; gas prices at a 22-month high of $3.63/gal signal coming inflationary pressure. BlackRock froze redemptions from a $26B fund, indicating private market stress.
regional_summaries.Europe: Belgium was paralyzed by a 24-hour general strike grounding all flights and causing an estimated EUR 25M daily loss. Denmark called snap elections for March 24, capitalizing on the Greenland standoff with Trump. France announced its largest naval deployment in decades — 10 warships including the Charles de Gaulle — to reopen Hormuz after Iranian drones struck EU member Cyprus. UK gilt yields rose 41bps month-on-month to 4.83%, and Italian BTP yields climbed 43bps to 3.80%, reflecting war-driven inflation expectations.
regional_summaries.East Asia: North Korea fired 10 ballistic missiles during US-South Korea Freedom Shield exercises, the largest single salvo on record. The Nikkei fell 3.24% weekly on energy import concerns. South Korea's KOSPI dropped 1.75% amid DPRK tensions and Section 301 exposure. China's top graduates are shifting from tech and finance to manufacturing, signaling deepening industrial self-sufficiency ambitions as Beijing faces US overcapacity investigations across 21 sectors.
regional_summaries.South Asia: India's BSE Sensex fell 5.31% weekly — the sharpest selloff among major indices — driven by Iran-related oil spike exposure as India imports over 80% of its crude. Pakistan remains in active conflict after declaring war on Afghanistan, with its credit rating at CCC+/Caa1 and political risk premium at 900 bps. Bangladesh and Sri Lanka face secondary effects from the Hormuz closure, including stranded Iranian sailors creating diplomatic dilemmas.
regional_summaries.Middle East & North Africa: The region remains the epicenter of the global crisis. Iran is under sustained bombardment with its Supreme Leader killed and replaced by his more hardline son. The Strait of Hormuz is mined and effectively closed. UAE, Kuwait, and Qatar face near-total economic paralysis from chokepoint exposure of 93.8-100%. Israel has expanded its Lebanon war past the Litani River. Sudan's RSF drone campaign killed 200+ civilians since March 4, with UAE accused of enabling drone supply via Chad. Libya maintains elevated investment risk at 0.547.
regional_summaries.Sub-Saharan Africa: The DRC conflict continues with M23 controlling eastern provinces including Goma and Bukavu. The US sanctioned Rwanda's Defence Force and four senior officers for supporting M23, threatening Rwanda's foreign investment climate. Critical minerals (cobalt, coltan, tin) supply chains remain disrupted. Ethiopia faces selective default (SD/Ca/RD credit) with Tigray conflict resumption and elections scheduled for June 1. Nigeria and Angola benefit from high oil prices as Atlantic coast exporters outside the Hormuz disruption zone.
regional_summaries.Latin America: Trump launched the 'Shield of Americas' anti-cartel military alliance with 15 Latin American nations and formally recognized Venezuela's Delcy Rodriguez as head of state, signaling potential sanctions relief on Venezuelan oil — critical amid the Iran war supply disruption. OFAC issued a general license for Venezuelan-origin gold transactions. Argentina, now a net energy exporter via Vaca Muerta shale, stands to benefit from elevated oil prices despite inflation at 219.9% under Milei's shock therapy.
regional_summaries.Central Asia: The region remains relatively insulated from the immediate Hormuz crisis but faces secondary effects from elevated energy prices and trade route disruptions. Kazakhstan benefits as a non-OPEC oil producer with pipeline access to China and Europe, though global demand uncertainty caps upside. Uzbekistan's reform trajectory continues with limited direct exposure to current conflicts.
regional_summaries.Southeast Asia: Vietnam holds general elections on March 15 for its 16th National Assembly, with 73.5 million voters casting ballots. The one-party system ensures policy continuity, but US Section 301 investigations create trade policy uncertainty for Vietnamese exporters. Indonesia's JCI fell 3.05% in a single day — the sharpest daily decline among major indices — on oil import costs. Singapore faces extreme chokepoint exposure (0.940) as a Hormuz-dependent trade hub. A $674M green energy MoU was signed in South Korea.
regional_summaries.Oceania: Australia's S&P/ASX 200 fell 2.64% weekly as energy import costs rise despite domestic production. The IEA SPR release includes Australian contributions. New Zealand's 10Y yield stable at 4.69%. Both nations are exposed to the Section 301 forced labor investigation as US trading partners, though direct trade impacts are expected to be minimal.

market_context.summary: Global markets remain under severe stress from the Iran war and Hormuz blockade. Oil above $100/bbl is the dominant driver, with Brent at $103.86 and WTI at $99.34 (up 8.86% weekly). Gold hit $5,019.70/oz — record territory — as safe-haven demand surges. Equities sold off broadly, led by India's Sensex (-5.31% weekly) and Japan's Nikkei (-3.24% weekly). Seven central banks (Fed, ECB, BoJ, BoE, BoC, RBA, SNB) decide rates March 16-19, creating the most volatile forex week expected in Q1 2026.

notable_moves.driver: Hormuz blockade persists; Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal destroyed; IEA 400M barrel SPR release insufficient to offset ~8M bpd supply cut
Iran war supply disruption and geopolitical risk premium; $99.34/bbl approaching $100 barrier
Record high at $5,019.70/oz on safe-haven demand amid Middle East war and trade policy uncertainty
Sharp selloff on Iran-related geopolitical tensions and oil import cost spike; India imports 80%+ of crude
Energy import dependency concerns and DPRK missile tensions; yen weakness amplifies import costs
Single-day decline on oil import cost surge; IDR at 16,900 per USD
Surged past 31% amid political turmoil — highest since at least 2010
Monthly rise of 41bps to 4.83% on war-driven inflation expectations
Daily rise of 18bps to 9.21% — largest single-day move among tracked sovereigns

watchlist_updates.situation: Under sustained US-Israeli bombardment entering third week. 6,000+ targets struck. Supreme Leader killed and replaced by hardline son. Kharg Island oil terminal destroyed. Economy in freefall.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei installed, vowing continued resistance and Hormuz blockade. Civilian death toll reached 1,348 with 17,000+ injured. Oil export infrastructure destroyed.

watchlist_updates.situation: Chokepoint exposure at maximum (1.000). Under direct Iranian missile attacks on Al Dhafra base and cities. Aviation disrupted with 7,700+ delays.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Continued direct military threat from Iran. GDP trend remains in strong decline. No pipeline alternative to Hormuz available.

watchlist_updates.situation: Near-total economic paralysis from Hormuz closure with 93.8% chokepoint exposure — highest among tracked countries. Ali Al Salem Air Base struck by IRGC.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: No improvement in ability to export oil. GDP trend remains in strong decline with no pipeline alternative.

watchlist_updates.situation: World's largest LNG exporter completely offline due to Hormuz mines. 91.6% chokepoint exposure. Al Udeid Air Base struck.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Qatar F-15QA jets intercepted Iranian bombers two minutes from striking US base. North Field LNG expansion timeline at risk. GDP trend in strong decline.

watchlist_updates.situation: Multi-front war: Iran strikes and Lebanon ground offensive past Litani River. 680+ killed in Lebanon campaign. GDP trend in strong decline.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Dramatic expansion of Lebanon operations. IDF advanced past Litani River. International condemnation growing. Hezbollah retaliating for Khamenei killing.

watchlist_updates.situation: Russia-Ukraine war Year 5. Peace talks stalled at '90% complete.' Russia launching large-scale drone and missile barrages. CC/Ca credit rating. 34% of GDP on defense.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Russia hit Ukraine with large-scale barrage killing at least 10 in Kharkiv. Peace talks remain stalled as Russia benefits from oil windfall. Zelenskiy spoke with Saudi Crown Prince MBS.

watchlist_updates.situation: Declared war on Afghanistan with airstrikes on Kabul and Kandahar. Nuclear-armed nation in active conflict. CCC+/Caa1 credit rating.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Conflict continues with no de-escalation signals. Investment risk elevated at 0.559. Political risk premium at 900 bps.

watchlist_updates.situation: Civil war continues between military junta and resistance forces. Economy in severe contraction. Humanitarian crisis deepening.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: No improvement. Investment risk at 0.639. Political risk premium at 850 bps. Inflation at 31%.

watchlist_updates.situation: Political risk premium at 1,900 bps — highest among tracked countries. Oil windfall from $100+ prices offsets sanctions pressure. Investment risk at 0.661.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Russia emerges as early economic winner from Iran war via oil revenue surge. Peace talks stalled in Russia's favor. US distracted by Middle East. Launched major Ukraine barrage.

watchlist_updates.situation: Selective default (SD/Ca/RD). TPLF-government clashes resumed. Political risk premium at 1,473 bps.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Elections scheduled for June 1, 2026. Tigray conflict resumption adds instability. Investment risk at 0.450 approaching warning threshold.

watchlist_updates.situation: Extreme chokepoint exposure at 0.940 as a Hormuz-dependent trade hub. GDP trend declining.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: No resolution to Hormuz crisis. Trade hub operations under strain from shipping disruptions and elevated insurance costs.

watchlist_updates.situation: M23 controls eastern provinces including Goma and Bukavu. Critical minerals supply chain disrupted. US sanctions on Rwanda escalate regional tensions.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: M23 handed 5,000+ FARDC prisoners to ICRC. Congolese army conducted drone strikes on M23 officials in Goma. Investment risk at 0.493 approaching warning threshold.

watchlist_updates.situation: Elevated investment risk at 0.573. Inflation at 28.3%. Political risk premium at 895 bps.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Condition improved from previous assessment. Oil import costs rising but Suez Canal revenues partially offset. GDP trend declining.

watchlist_updates.situation: 10Y bond yields surged past 31% — highest since at least 2010. Political turmoil elevating market stress.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Bond yields at crisis levels amid political uncertainty. Currency weakness continuing with TRY at 44.19 per USD.

watchlist_updates.situation: Hyperinflation at 219.9%. Political risk premium at 950 bps. Investment risk at 0.449 approaching warning threshold.
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Becoming net energy exporter for first time via Vaca Muerta — benefits from elevated oil prices. Milei reform credibility rising despite -1.3% GDP.
